prompts: The human endocrine system consists of various glands throughout the body that secrete hormones to regulate physiological functions. Key glands include the pituitary gland, thyroid gland, adrenal glands, pancreas, and reproductive glands (ovaries and testes). The pituitary gland, often referred to as the master gland, influences other endocrine glands and regulates growth and metabolism. The thyroid gland controls metabolic rate, while the adrenal glands produce stress-related hormones like cortisol and adrenaline. The pancreas regulates blood sugar levels with insulin and glucagon secretion. Hormones circulate in the bloodstream, enabling precise regulation and coordination of bodily functions.
